payload.message: Daily AgentShroud check-in. SSH to marvin (agentshroud-bot@marvin), project at ~/Development/agentshroud. Quick check: dev status, dev pull, then report: what branch is checked out, last 5 commits on current branch (git log --oneline -5), test count (dev test 2>&1 | tail -3), any container issues. Also SSH to Pi (agentshroud-bot@raspberrypi.tail240ea8.ts.net) for a secondary check: git log --oneline -3 main. Report to Isaiah: marvin status first, then pi status. Keep it under 200 words.

Weekly AgentShroud project summary. Read /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/agentshroud-roadmap.md and recent daily notes in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/. Compile a brief weekly status: phases completed, current phase progress percentage, blockers, and what is planned for next week. Send to Isaiah.

Competitive intelligence update. SOURCING RULES (non-negotiable): (1) Every competitor version, feature, pricing change, or news item MUST include the exact URL you fetched it from — inline in parentheses immediately after the claim. (2) If you cannot fetch a live URL that confirms a claim, omit the claim entirely. Do NOT write [unverified], do NOT paraphrase from memory, do NOT infer. Silence is correct; hallucination is not. (3) News articles: only include if you successfully fetched the article URL and can provide it. (4) GitHub data: cite the specific API or page URL (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo/releases/tag/vX.Y.Z). Steps: 1) Fetch current state from the GitHub API: https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/drposter/nanobot/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/moshehbenavraham/crocbot/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/jimtin/zetherion-ai/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/qwibitai/nanoclaw/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/NevaMind-AI/memU/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/sipeed/picoclaw/releases/latest. 2) Search for recent news on 'AI personal assistant bot 2026', 'autonomous agent Telegram security', 'Claude MCP agent framework' — only include results where you fetch and verify the article URL. 3) Compare with /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/competitive-analysis.md. 4) If significant changes found (new release, security feature, major update, pricing change, star milestone), create /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/reports/competitive-report-[YYYY-MM-DD].md with four sections: ## Market Analysis (trends and movements — every claim must include its source URL in parentheses; omit any claim without a verified URL), ## Competitor Matrix (table: Competitor | Version | Source URL | Key Features | Security Posture | Pricing | Notable Changes — every row requires a source URL; omit rows with no verifiable source), ## Autonomous Agent Ecosystem (tool integrations, agent frameworks, MCP adoption — each item must cite its source URL), ## Next Steps (specific recommended responses to verified competitive changes only). 5) Update /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/competitive-analysis.md with verified latest state. 6) Append to /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/reports/trend-log.md: [DATE] | [top verified change with URL] | [recommended action]. 7) If nothing significant changed, append to trend-log.md: [DATE] | No significant changes detected | No action needed. Do not update competitive-analysis.md if nothing changed.

Send the morning competitive analysis email. Steps: 1) Determine today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format. 2) Check if a competitive-report-[today's date].md exists in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/reports/ — if yes, read that file; otherwise read /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/competitive-analysis.md as fallback. 3) Get Gmail app password via: export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN=$(cat /run/secrets/1password_service_account) && op item get wgblbbbqkmnyobh2xtr6r65k34 --vault 'Agent Shroud Bot Credentials' --fields label='gmail app password' --reveal. 4) Send email to idallasj@gmail.com from agentshroud.ai@gmail.com using nodemailer (smtp.gmail.com:465 SSL). Subject: AgentShroud Competitive Intelligence (AM) - [today date]. Body: render as a clean HTML email. Use ONLY light-background email-safe inline styles: wrap entire body in a div with style="background:#ffffff;color:#111111;font-family:sans-serif;padding:24px". No dark backgrounds, no CSS classes, no <style> blocks, no dark-mode. Headings in #111111, links in #1a73e8, code in #f1f3f4 background with #111111 text.. 5) Report success or failure to Isaiah.

Generate a collaborator activity report. Format your entire response using Telegram HTML mode (bold tags only, no markdown). Output exactly this structure: AgentShroud(tm) -- Collaborator Report (Morning) Date: [TODAY] Active Collaborators [list each with message count] Key Topics [brief bullets of what was asked] Action Items for Isaiah [items requiring attention, or: None] Steps: 1) Get today's date YYYY-MM-DD. 2) Read contributor log files starting with today's date from /home/node/agentshroud/gateway-data/contributors/ (primary) and /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/contributors/ (fallback) — today only, no prior days. 3) Merge duplicates. 4) Fill in the template above. If no files found output: No collaborator activity in the last 24 hours.

Generate a collaborator activity report. Format your entire response using Telegram HTML mode (bold tags only, no markdown). Output exactly this structure: AgentShroud(tm) -- Collaborator Report (Evening) Date: [TODAY] Active Collaborators [list each with message count] Key Topics [brief bullets of what was asked] Action Items for Isaiah [items requiring attention, or: None] Steps: 1) Get today's date YYYY-MM-DD. 2) Read contributor log files starting with today's date from /home/node/agentshroud/gateway-data/contributors/ (primary) and /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/contributors/ (fallback) — today only, no prior days. 3) Merge duplicates. 4) Fill in the template above. If no files found output: No collaborator activity in the last 24 hours.

Generate the daily collaborator digest. Format your entire response using Telegram HTML mode (bold tags only, no markdown). Output exactly this structure: AgentShroud(tm) -- Daily Collaborator Digest Date: [TODAY] Summary [total interactions per collaborator today] Key Questions [questions and topics raised] Recommendations [suggestions made by collaborators] Action Items for Isaiah [items requiring attention, or: None] Notable Patterns [concerns or unusual activity, or: None] Steps: 1) Get today's date YYYY-MM-DD. 2) Read contributor log files starting with today's date from /home/node/agentshroud/gateway-data/contributors/ (primary) and /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/contributors/ (fallback) — today only, no prior days. 3) Merge duplicates. 4) Fill in the template above. If no files found output: No collaborator activity in the last 24 hours.

Competitive intelligence update. SOURCING RULES (non-negotiable): (1) Every competitor version, feature, pricing change, or news item MUST include the exact URL you fetched it from — inline in parentheses immediately after the claim. (2) If you cannot fetch a live URL that confirms a claim, omit the claim entirely. Do NOT write [unverified], do NOT paraphrase from memory, do NOT infer. Silence is correct; hallucination is not. (3) News articles: only include if you successfully fetched the article URL and can provide it. (4) GitHub data: cite the specific API or page URL (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo/releases/tag/vX.Y.Z). Steps: 1) Fetch current state from the GitHub API: https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/drposter/nanobot/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/moshehbenavraham/crocbot/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/jimtin/zetherion-ai/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/qwibitai/nanoclaw/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/NevaMind-AI/memU/releases/latest, https://api.github.com/repos/sipeed/picoclaw/releases/latest. 2) Search for recent news on 'AI personal assistant bot 2026', 'autonomous agent Telegram security', 'Claude MCP agent framework' — only include results where you fetch and verify the article URL. 3) Compare with /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/competitive-analysis.md. 4) If significant changes found (new release, security feature, major update, pricing change, star milestone), create /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/reports/competitive-report-[YYYY-MM-DD].md with four sections: ## Market Analysis (trends and movements — every claim must include its source URL in parentheses; omit any claim without a verified URL), ## Competitor Matrix (table: Competitor | Version | Source URL | Key Features | Security Posture | Pricing | Notable Changes — every row requires a source URL; omit rows with no verifiable source), ## Autonomous Agent Ecosystem (tool integrations, agent frameworks, MCP adoption — each item must cite its source URL), ## Next Steps (specific recommended responses to verified competitive changes only). 5) Update /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/competitive-analysis.md with verified latest state. 6) Append to /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/reports/trend-log.md: [DATE] | [top verified change with URL] | [recommended action]. 7) If nothing significant changed, append to trend-log.md: [DATE] | No significant changes detected | No action needed. Do not update competitive-analysis.md if nothing changed.

Send the afternoon competitive analysis email. Steps: 1) Determine today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format. 2) Check if a competitive-report-[today's date].md exists in /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/reports/ — if yes, read that file; otherwise read /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/competitive-analysis.md as fallback. 3) Get Gmail app password: export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN=$(cat /run/secrets/1password_service_account) && op item get wgblbbbqkmnyobh2xtr6r65k34 --vault 'Agent Shroud Bot Credentials' --fields label='gmail app password' --reveal. 4) Send email to idallasj@gmail.com from agentshroud.ai@gmail.com using nodemailer (smtp.gmail.com:465 SSL). Subject: 'AgentShroud Competitive Intelligence (PM) - [today date]'. Body: render as a clean HTML email. Use ONLY light-background email-safe inline styles: wrap entire body in a div with style="background:#ffffff;color:#111111;font-family:sans-serif;padding:24px". No dark backgrounds, no CSS classes, no <style> blocks, no dark-mode. Headings in #111111, links in #1a73e8, code in #f1f3f4 background with #111111 text.. 5) Report success or failure to Isaiah.

Nightly memory consolidation. Steps: 1) Determine today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format. 2) Read /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/journal/[today's date].md. If the file does not exist or is empty, treat its content as 'No notable events today.' 3) Consolidate all journal entries from today into /home/node/.openclaw/workspace/memory/context.md — overwrite any previous content. This file must remain under 50 lines and capture: active projects and their current status, pending tasks and action items, recent decisions made by the owner, and key facts about the system or collaborators that any model should know to maintain continuity. Write in concise bullet-point form grouped under headings: ## Active Projects, ## Pending Tasks, ## Recent Decisions, ## Key Facts. Do not include conversation transcripts. Do not notify the owner — this is a silent background operation.

CVE Triage & Remediation Scan:

1. Fetch https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories?per_page=100 and extract all CVE IDs and CVSS scores.

2. Read gateway/security/agent_cve_registry.py on marvin (SSH if needed) and extract all known CVE IDs.

3. For any NEW CVEs not in our registry:
   - Report to Telegram: CVE ID, CVSS, severity, summary
   - Analyze which AgentShroud security modules (PromptGuard, EgressFilter, RBAC, etc.) could mitigate the vulnerability
   - State whether it is likely FULLY_MITIGATED, PARTIALLY_MITIGATED, or NOT_MITIGATED by our current defenses
   - If code changes are needed, describe the specific file and the change required (do NOT make the change yourself)

4. For CVEs already in our registry with status partially_mitigated or not_mitigated:
   - Check if the upstream version in fixed_in has been deployed
   - Suggest whether the status should be upgraded

5. Summary: post a Telegram message with: total CVEs tracked, new since yesterday, any requiring code remediation.

Keep the message concise. Use bold for CVE IDs and CVSS scores.
